skill.name: at-dispatch-v2
skill.description: Guides conversion of PyTorch AT_DISPATCH macros to AT_DISPATCH_V2 in ATen C++ kernel code.
skill.summary: Convert PyTorch AT_DISPATCH macros to the AT_DISPATCH_V2 API.
skill.author: pytorch

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many Markdown inline-code examples as Ruby shell backticks and flagged Dispatch text as weak cryptography. Manual review found only instructional C++ macro examples and no executable scripts, network access, credential handling, prompt injection, or malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Spans Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command detections are Markdown inline code and fenced C++ examples that document PyTorch macros. They do not invoke a shell, define scripts, or execute user-controlled input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged backticks are Markdown formatting around C++ symbols and examples. No shell interpreter, process execution API, or generated command string is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Matches Dispatch Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections appear to match substrings in the PyTorch Dispatch wording and include path. The file contains no cryptographic algorithm use or security-sensitive hashing logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe AT_DISPATCH conversion and a C++ include directive. There is no DES, MD5, SHA1, cipher setup, or cryptographic operation in context.

content.value_statement: Legacy AT_DISPATCH macros are tedious to migrate by hand. This skill guides precise conversion to AT_DISPATCH_V2 for ATen C++ kernels.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to convert legacy AT_DISPATCH macros to AT_DISPATCH_V2.
Maps legacy macro families to AT_DISPATCH_V2 type group arguments.
Shows how to wrap lambdas with AT_WRAP for comma-safe macro expansion.
Identifies where to add the ATen Dispatch_v2.h include.
Provides examples for floating, integral, complex, and mixed dispatch patterns.
limitations: Does not compile, test, or benchmark converted PyTorch code.
Focuses on ATen C++ dispatch macros, not general PyTorch Python APIs.
Requires the user to provide the target source file or macro snippet.
May need human review for unusual macro nesting or custom dispatch wrappers.

use_cases.title: Migrate Native Kernels
use_cases.description: Convert ATen native kernel dispatch macros while preserving scalar type coverage.

use_cases.title: Review Dispatch Changes
use_cases.description: Check that converted AT_DISPATCH_V2 calls use the correct argument order and type groups.

use_cases.title: Modernize Extension Code
use_cases.description: Adapt dispatch macro patterns in custom native operators to the newer v2 API style.

prompt_templates.title: Convert One Macro
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner conversion of a single dispatch call.
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this AT_DISPATCH macro to AT_DISPATCH_V2 and explain each argument change.

prompt_templates.title: Update a Kernel File
prompt_templates.scenario: Convert all dispatch macros in one ATen source file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Find every AT_DISPATCH macro in this file, add the v2 include if needed, and convert each call.

prompt_templates.title: Check Type Coverage
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this AT_DISPATCH_V2 conversion and verify that all original scalar types are still covered.

prompt_templates.title: Handle Complex Lambdas
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced conversion with nested templates or commas.
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert these dispatch macros with complex lambda bodies and ensure AT_WRAP covers the full lambda.

output_examples.input: A legacy AT_DISPATCH_ALL_TYPES_AND3 macro in an ATen CUDA kernel.
output_examples.output: A converted AT_DISPATCH_V2 call with the dtype and name first, the lambda wrapped, and extra scalar types listed last.

output_examples.input: A review request for a dispatch macro migration.
output: Confirms the v2 header is present.
Flags missing AT_WRAP usage or misplaced individual types.

best_practices: Keep the original scalar type coverage visible before editing the macro.
Wrap the entire lambda with AT_WRAP, especially when the body contains commas.
Review the final argument order before accepting the conversion.
anti_patterns: Do not remove existing Dispatch.h includes unless the surrounding file proves they are unused.
Do not wrap individual scalar types with AT_EXPAND.
Do not convert unrelated macros while reviewing one dispatch migration.

faq.question: What does this skill convert?
faq.answer: It converts PyTorch AT_DISPATCH macro calls to the AT_DISPATCH_V2 format used in ATen C++ code.

faq.question: Does it run or compile the code?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides editing guidance and asks the user to verify builds separately.

faq.question: Why is AT_WRAP required?
faq.answer: AT_WRAP protects lambda bodies that contain commas so macro parsing remains correct.

faq.question: Can it handle complex type dispatch?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for all-types, floating, integral, complex, and combined macro families.

faq.question: Does it support Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What should users review manually?
faq.answer: Users should verify includes, type group mapping, lambda wrapping, and the final build result.
